channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Female veterans find solace in surf with 'life-changing' therapy
item.description: A Queensland woman has launched a program designed specifically for women veterans to improve their mental health.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ross and Sarah Howells

media:group.media:description: Tammy Grant hopes to expand her surf therapy program to help more veterans.

item.title: Could a roof sprinkler save your home in a bushfire?
item.description: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.
item.dc:creator: Bec Pridham

media:group.media:description: Aerial view of Ivan and Pam Walker's coastal home on 21 January, 2026, saved from the Stieglitz bushfire near St Helens, Tasmania. The recent Stieglitz bushfire burned right up to the couple's fence, but did not reach any structures. The Walkers installed sprinklers on their roof to help defend their home.

item.title: F1 driver smashes into groundhog during Canadian Grand Prix session
item.description: A groundhog is killed and an F1 car heavily damaged after a collision during practice for the Canadian Grand Prix.

media:group.media:description: Alex Albon smashes into the wall after hitting a groundhog.

item.title: Albany's hidden WWII fuel tanks transformed into global street art gallery
item.description: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

media:group.media:description: The art reflects off the surface of large pools of water inside the tanks.

item.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south
item.description: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney’s south, with police attempting to conduct a welfare check at one of the victim’s homes minutes before the incident

media:group.media:description: Three people have died in a road crash in Sans Souci in Sydney's south.

item.title: Top End tourism and hospitality businesses seek lifeline amid rising costs
item.description: A popular Darwin restaurant has closed down and the owner of another local hospitality business is asking for COVID-era support measures to be brought back as costs rise and visitor numbers fall.

media:group.media:description: Nicho Peters says there are a lot of challenges facing Top End small businesses.

item.title: Giant iceberg with shipping containers spotted off Antarctica
item.description: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: A SkySat satellite image taken on February 4 showing the shipping containers on top of the iceberg.

item.title: Black mould and broken toilets in 'dismal' government-owned homes
item.description: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.
item.dc:creator: Dunja Karagic and Giulia Bertoglio

media:group.media:description: The inside of a house in Kalumburu, WA.

item.title: Health Minister denies health cuts, despite asking department to find $700m in cuts
item.description: Health groups fear the impact of hundreds of millions of dollars in cuts expected to be found in the Health Department as part of Treasurer Eric Abetz's state budget operational efficiencies program.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: A generic image of a doctor or a nurse.

item.title: UN climate expert goes back to school to address some burning questions
item.description: Primary school students in regional Victoria have their climate change questions answered.
item.dc:creator: Annie Brown

media:group.media:description: Elisa Morgera is the UN special rapporteur on climate and human rights.

item.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria
item.description: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Keane

media:group.media:description: South Australian football legend Craig Bradley during a State of Origin match against Victoria.

item.title: Tony Albert's form of optimism is a lesson for us all
item.description: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".
item.dc:creator: Virginia Trioli

media:group.media:description: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".

item.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?
item.description: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.
item.dc:creator: James Wakelin

media:group.media:description: The rise of minor parties like the Greens and One Nation is dismantling Australia's two-party system, a pollster says.

item.title: Woman and baby die after highway crash north of Cairns
item.description: Police said initial investigations indicated a white Mitsubishi Triton ute struck a red Mazda MX-5 before colliding with a white MG3 hatchback.

media:group.media:description: Police, paramedics and firefighters were called to the scene on Friday.

item.title: Little penguin numbers climb on Kangaroo Island despite algal bloom impact
item.description: Little penguin numbers on Kangaroo Island have rebounded, but estimates show there are only about 700 adult breeders after a harmful algal bloom.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Horn

media:group.media:description: A little penguin was sighted at Vivonne Bay in the 2025 survey.

item.title: Victoria's leaders pitch apprenticeship academy, regional infrastructure
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan and Opposition Leader Jess Wilson will deliver their pre-election promises as both parties hold their state conferences.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: Jess Wilson and Jacinta Allan shake hands in Wilson's first appearance in parliament as leader in 2025. The two leaders are outlining their respective election platforms over the weekend.

item.title: Pendlebury's greatest role on the path to record was restoring the Pies
item.description: Scott Pendlebury's record-breaking career has spanned almost five separate Collingwood eras, with each one helping put the club back near the top of the AFL's pecking order.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Scott Pendlebury's storied career has seen Collingwood go from a premiership afterthought to a legitimate destination club.

item.title: Does Queensland's premier have his attack dog on a leash?
item.description: Queensland's deputy premier made a 'dick' joke about his political opponent this week, but the difference in his approach and the premier's raises questions, writes political reporter Jack McKay.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Jarrod Bleijie's attacking approach differs from that taken by David Crisafulli.

item.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in DRC to 'very high'
item.description: The World Health Organization raises its assessment of the outbreak going nation-wide as a province in the Congo's east bans funerals and suspends the local football league.

media:group.media:description: Red Cross workers disinfect Rwampara's general hospital before handling a body on Thursday.

item.title: Dangerous area in Sydney for rock fishers trials life jacket library
item.description: Rock fishers in Sydney's east can now borrow and return life jackets for free in a bid to help reduce the number of drownings in the area.
item.dc:creator: Danuta Kozaki

media:group.media:description: Surf Life Saving NSW and Randwick City Council have established a free Life Jacket Library in Maroubra as part of SLNSW Rock Fishing Safety Project.

item.title: Shipping containers of water slide parts left idle as park plans axed
item.description: A developer blames the Iran war for his decision to axe a water park and resort project in regional Queensland despite already spending $21 million.
item.dc:creator: Adam Stephen and Sharnie Kim

media:group.media:description: Richard Cheong's home of 31 years backs onto an axed water park and resort development at Kewarra Beach in Cairns.

item.title: Nine charts that reveal who pays more under Labor's tax changes
item.description: Labor's capital gains tax changes have set off a firestorm of debate about the implications for small businesses, start-ups, share owners and plenty more. Here's who will really pay more.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers set off a firestorm of debate with proposed tax changes.

item.title: Building townhouses on a residential Canberra block could soon be easier
item.description: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Gore and Adam Shirley

media:group.media:description: The ACT government's proposed changes are aimed at increasing the number of terrace houses, townhouses, and low-rise apartments.

item.title: IVF inquiry hears harrowing stories of fertility treatment outside big cities
item.description: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Blandin de Chalain, Lara Webster, and Brigitte Murphy

media:group.media:description: Sophie Fletcher has spent countless hours on the road travelling to metro centres to receive care.

item.title: 'Life is football' here but as a favourite son heads home, it needs help
item.description: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.
item.dc:creator: Paul Kennedy and Paul Johnson

media:group.media:description: Willie Rioli Jr takes a breather on his return to Tiwi Bombers footy.
